name: Fingerroot
summary: Internal cross-linking supports Fingerroot through compounds such as pinostrobin, pinocembrin, panduratin a.
description: Internal cross-linking supports Fingerroot through compounds such as pinostrobin, pinocembrin, panduratin a.
contraindications: diabetes medication because fingerroot's pinostrobin and pinocembrin inhibit carbohydrate-digesting enzymes and increase insulin secretion which may cause an additive blood-glucose-lowering effect
CYP1A2-metabolized medication such as caffeine or theophylline or clozapine because pinocembrin is a potent competitive CYP1A2 inhibitor with predicted clinical interaction potential
pregnancy without clinician guidance because fingerroot's traditional use for easing menstrual cramps suggests uterine smooth-muscle activity
known allergy to Zingiberaceae family plants because Boesenbergia rotunda is in the ginger family

Traditional usages, chemical metabolites, pharmacological activities, and pharmacokinetics of Boesenbergia rotunda (L.) Mansf.: a comprehensive review

dosage: Preparation-specific; use standardized product label for Root/rhizome preparations are commonly used as teas and require source-backed dosage review before therapeutic claims.
typical_dosage: Preparation-specific; use standardized product label for Root/rhizome preparations are commonly used as teas and require source-backed dosage review before therapeutic claims.
